title: Repair the hosted Meta panel facing and promote the recovered toolkit lane
summary: 0.4.13 turns the recovered Meta video-player lane into a usable local foundation. The hosted Meta proof panel now faces the operator instead of rendering only from the rear, the recovered Unity toolkit lane now exports a readable self-contained video-player package from the repo-local ghost training project, and the catalog sync path now replaces stale package folders so the live theme root carries one current package instead of an archaeological pile of hashes.
reasoning: The player core and the local hosted control slab were already working, but Meta remained stuck in a half-recovered state: the hosted panel layout pointed the wrong way, the toolkit export lane still depended on a recently moved Unity tool path, and the live toolkit root still held only an older broken video-player package. This slice reconnects those seams without widening into unrelated interaction work, so Meta can become a real current release lane inside the cleaned repo structure.

changes.summary: Fixes the hosted Meta proof layout so the control panel follows the screen on the operator-facing side instead of appearing only from the rear.
changes.reasoning: The export could already produce a readable panel, but the live hosted runtime still rotated the proof control surface behind the screen.

changes.summary: Repairs the recovered toolkit exporter so the local video-player proof scene is used as the source for the self-contained Meta video-player package and preserves the proof control bindings during export.
changes.reasoning: The recovered package needed to bake from a real local proof scene with explicit semantic controls, not from the older package-scene placeholder path.

changes.summary: Makes the Meta toolkit sync path replace stale folders and adds fallback resolution so the runtime and host-package tooling can find the current installed video-player package even after the package hash changes.
changes.reasoning: The old toolkit root accumulated broken historical package ids, which caused drift between the export that looked right and the package that live VaM could actually import.

changes.summary: Keeps the active Meta export project in `fa` and the shared `unity-editor-bridge` package in `frameangel_tools`, with the recovered local scripts targeting those new homes.
changes.reasoning: The operator needs the recovered Unity tools to open and export from their current landing zones without bouncing through historical paths.

verification: Build and deploy the versioned `fa_cua_player.0.4.13.dll` and matching `fa_player_asset.0.4.13.assetbundle` through the player foundation script.

Sync the repaired Meta toolkit theme catalog into `build\meta_toolkit_catalog\theme_00` and deploy it into `F:\sim\vam\Custom\PluginData\FrameAngel\meta_toolkit_demo\theme_00` as a clean current root.
Confirm the deployed theme root exposes the current self-contained `faipe_meta_contentuiexample_videoplayer_*` package with the readable five-control snapshot.
In live VaM, attach the 0.4.13 player plugin and verify the hosted Meta proof panel faces the operator and no longer renders as a rear-only dark slab.
knownIssues: The recovered Unity project is now in the correct repo lane, but its authoring source still needs a later pass to trim down historical generated assets before it becomes a small tidy project.
This slice repairs the current Meta video-player surface and deploy path; A-B loop retrieval, playlists, and richer Meta interaction still remain separate work.
The current distribution player still ships the basic screen-core product surface; broader shell-family polish and Pro-lane assembly continue separately.
